full_response:
First, recall the constraint:  
Ben owns only one copy of each of the five gifts {apple, banana, cherry, durian, egg}.  
Therefore two friends can be made simultaneously happy iff the sets of gifts they demand are disjoint (no gift is required by both).  
(The trivial set ∅ never causes a conflict.)

We check each listed pair.

A. Alice & Bob  
 S₁ = {apple, banana}, S₂ = {banana, cherry, egg}  
 Common gift = banana ≠ ∅ ⇒ conflict.  
 Impossible.

B. Alice & Daisy  
 S₁ = {apple, banana}, S₄ = {durian, egg}  
 S₁ ∩ S₄ = ∅ ⇒ no conflict.  
 Give apple, banana to Alice and durian, egg to Daisy.  
 Possible.

C. Charlie & Daisy  
 S₃ = {apple, banana, cherry, durian, egg}, S₄ = {durian, egg}  
 Common gifts = {durian, egg} ≠ ∅ ⇒ conflict.  
 Impossible.

D. Charlie & Eve  
 S₃ = {apple, banana, cherry, durian, egg}, S₅ = ∅  
 S₃ ∩ S₅ = ∅ (Eve wants nothing) ⇒ no conflict.  
 Give all five gifts to Charlie; Eve needs none.  
 Possible.

\boxed{\text{Pairs B and D can be made simultaneously happy}}  

Confidence: 0.83
extracted_answer: \text{Pairs B and D can be made simultaneously happy}
